Implements a singleton event loop for the JerryScript mbed environment.

# Purpose
This code is part of a C++ library that provides functionality for managing an event loop using the `jerryscript-mbed-event-loop` library. It defines a singleton instance of the `EventLoop` class within the `mbed::js` namespace. The function `event_loop()` is a utility function that starts the event loop by calling the `go()` method on the singleton `EventLoop` instance. This code is intended to be used as part of a larger application where an event-driven architecture is required, and it is not an executable on its own.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `jerryscript-mbed-event-loop/EventLoop.h`


# Functions

---
### event\_loop<!-- {{#callable:mbed::js::event_loop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/mbedos5/jerryscript-mbed/jerryscript-mbed-event-loop/source/EventLoop.cpp#L22>)

Starts the event loop by calling the `go` method on the singleton instance of `EventLoop`.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call `EventLoop::getInstance()` to get the singleton instance of `EventLoop`.
    - Invoke the `go` method on the retrieved `EventLoop` instance to start the event loop.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function has a `void` return type.
